channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran if it doesn't agree to nuclear deal
item.description: “If they don’t make a deal, there will be bombing and it will be bombing the likes of which they have never seen before,” Trump said in a comment aired Sunday on NBC News.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's attorney general on Sunday asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters, a request that came hours before President Donald Trump's ally planned the giveaway at an evening rally.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Richard Chamberlain, TV actor who starred in 'Dr. Kildare,' dies at 90
item.description: Richard Chamberlain, the handsome hero of the 1960s television series “Dr. Kildare” who found a second career as “king of the miniseries,” has died at age 90.
item.dc:creator: Bob Thomas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tsunami warning lifted after 7.1 earthquake near Tonga in South Pacific; no initial damage reports
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A strong 7.1 magnitude earthquake hit near Tonga, prompting an initial tsunami warning that was later lifted for the Pacific island country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Madison Booker and top-seeded Texas outlast SEC rival Tennessee 67-59 to reach Elite Eight
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Texas coach Vic Schaefer got the tense battle he expected between his defensive-minded Longhorns and the highest-scoring team in the country in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Comedian Paul Rodriguez arrested on drug charge in California, police say
item.description: Rodriguez, 70, was booked at the Burbank Police Jail for narcotics possession and later released with a citation to appear in court on April 25, Sgt. Turner elaborated.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Columbia University's interim president steps down and returns to former post
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University’s interim president Katrina Armstrong has resigned, returning to her post running the New York school’s medical center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: State Department sends Congress letter saying it's taking over USAID
item.description: A source tells NewsNation a team trying to organize support for victims of the Myanmar earthquake was told to leave the office at midday.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck vandals caught on video scrawling 'Nazis' and swastika: NYPD
item.description: The New York Police Department Hate Crime Task Force is investigating after two men were caught on camera carving "Nazis" and a swastika onto a Cybertruck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, WGN 9

item.title: Son's skeletal remains found in home of man who died in scuba accident days earlier: report
item.description: A Georgia man died in a scuba diving accident merely days before the skeletal remains of his son, who had been missing for four years, were found in his backyard.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former El Paso Morgan Stanley financial adviser sentenced for multi-million fraud scheme
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — A former Morgan Stanley financial adviser in El Paso was sentenced in federal court to 12 years in prison for committing a multi-million dollar fraud scheme, according to the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Western District of Texas. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Lights, Camera, Relocation: Utahns react as Sundance films its final scene in Utah
item.description: Park City businesses are reacting to Sundance's decision to move to another location, with residents confident that the city will continue to be a world-class destination for arts and entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Renisha Mall, ABC 4

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A congressional hearing Thursday reinforced the idea that the January midair collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter that killed all 67 people aboard both aircraft could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bestselling author of 'The Vampire Diaries' dies at 66
item.description: Bestselling author L.J. Smith, best known for the book series "The Vampire Diaries," which also became a TV hit, has died at 66.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Bride-to-be brutally attacked during bachelorette trip in Dallas
item.description: An Oklahoma bride-to-be's bachelorette trip turned into a nightmare and something she will never forget, all for the wrong reasons.
item.dc:creator: Sydnee Batzlaff/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-North Dakota lawmaker gets 10 years for going to Europe with intent to pay for sex with a minor
item.description: A once-powerful former North Dakota lawmaker was sentenced Wednesday to 10 years in prison for traveling to Europe with the intent to pay for sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, ABC 4

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Wednesday asked the Supreme Court to allow it to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sewage sludge can find a second life on farm fields. Here's how it's made
item.description: What goes down your toilet can end up on farm fields across the United States. Biosolids, or sewage sludge, are the solid byproducts of the wastewater treatment process. Rich in nutrients, they can be used as fertilizer on agricultural fields or compost on lawns.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Democrats demand answers from Trump team about war plans Signal chat
item.description: The top Democrats on the House’s Defense, Oversight, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence committees want answers from President Trump’s national security team, saying they’ve become “deeply troubled” after a journalist was unknowingly added to a group chat of senior officials discussing war plans.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump admin from deporting South Korean Columbia student
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday blocked the Trump administration from deporting a Columbia University student who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations at the school.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump officials' war planning group chat breach
item.description: Washington was rocked Monday by a truly extraordinary story. The editor in chief of The Atlantic, Jeffrey Goldberg, recounted how he had — apparently inadvertently — been added to a group chat featuring some of the most senior members of the United States government.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Jeffries calls for investigation of top Trump officials after bombshell military breach
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) is calling for Congress to investigate top Trump administration officials in the wake of a bombshell revelation that The Atlantic’s top editor was included in a group chat discussion detailing the Pentagon’s plans to strike Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9
